lines.text: (low, to himself) [quietly] That's an empty box, and it's reading fifty-one.
(low) This one too, right about the same.
(low) [slowly] Thirty kilos over the paper, on all four.
[deliberate] Nothing on this manifest breathes, gentlemen.

Tuesday the truck carries six boxes out on your paper. Six of us are in them. [pause] The load reads right because that dummy already weighs what a man weighs. They wave it through the gate and nobody counts us at the yard.
You understand there's no backing out once you're up there. [pause] Bad heart at four g's, your age, no doctor closer than a quarter million miles.
Boone, I've buried better men than me for worse reasons than this. Sit down or go home. Either's fine. [flatly] Quit standing there doing arithmetic on our behalf.
[angry] You're all insane. What do you need from me?
